preamble: The shift from the vowel [o] to the glottal fricative [h] represents an extreme form of vowel reduction and devoicing. In rapid or whispered speech, an unstressed [o] loses its vocal fold vibration entirely, leaving only the friction of air passing through the glottis.

examples.note: In rapid speech, unstressed vowels can become devoiced, particularly in Northern dialects.

sources: Arvaniti: The Phonetics of Modern Greek
